title: Runtime Dispatch Semantics
text: SIMD source files use SIMDe types functions, backend names refer native target-specific staged kernel objects. configuration, optional backend accepted compiler flag SIMDe header together define expected native SIMDe macro. example, AVX2 probe compiles -mavx2, includes <simde/x86/avx2.h>, requires SIMDE_X86_AVX2_NATIVE. AVX-512 similarly requires native F, BW, VL macros. Emscripten/webR, WebAssembly SIMD128 probe compiles -msimd128, includes <simde/wasm/simd128.h>, requires SIMDE_WASM_SIMD128_NATIVE. flags make compiler define target macros __AVX2__, __AVX512BW__, __wasm_simd128__. SIMDe maps SIMDE_ARCH_* SIMDE_*_NATIVE; SIMDe function bodies use native intrinsics macros fall back native macro absent. generated src/Makevars links staged objects package shared library dispatcher, CPU feature detection, R API compiled R’s ordinary src/Makevars path. installed diagnostics report backends passed SIMDe-native compile probe:

title: Runtime Dispatch Semantics
text: following benchmark evaluated article built. uses small input package checks remain fast, still exercises -process backend switching public API. switch applies full one-dimensional convolution demo: "auto" selects best backend compiled supported intersection also supports operation called. current ranking : Architecture guards mean x86 systems normally consider x86 backends, ARM systems normally consider NEON, webR/Emscripten builds can consider WebAssembly SIMD128. Scalar always available.
code: if (requireNamespace("bench", quietly = TRUE)) {   bench_x <- rep(as.raw(c(0, 1, 2, 3, 0, 255, 7, 0)), length.out = 2^20)    bench <- bench::mark(     scalar = {       simd_set_backend("scalar")       count_nonzero(bench_x)     },     auto = {       simd_set_backend("auto")       count_nonzero(bench_x)     },     iterations = 5,     check = TRUE   )    simd_set_backend("auto")   bench[, c("expression", "median", "itr/sec", "n_itr")] } #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   expression   median `itr/sec` #>   <bch:expr> <bch:tm>     <dbl> #> 1 scalar      330.4µs     2984. #> 2 auto         59.4µs    15254. if (requireNamespace("bench", quietly = TRUE)) {   bench_a <- runif(10000)   bench_b <- runif(100)    conv_bench <- bench::mark(     scalar = {       simd_set_backend("scalar")       convolve1d(bench_a, bench_b)     },     auto = {       simd_set_backend("auto")       convolve1d(bench_a, bench_b)     },     iterations = 5,     check = TRUE   )    simd_set_backend("auto")   conv_bench[, c("expression", "median", "itr/sec", "n_itr")] } #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   expression   median `itr/sec` #>   <bch:expr> <bch:tm>     <dbl> #> 1 scalar        431µs     2313. #> 2 auto          190µs     5215. avx512 > avx2 > sse41 > sse2 > neon > wasm_simd128 > scalar

title: Using RsimdDispatch in Other Packages
text: downstream package root: pkg omitted, package name read DESCRIPTION. prefix omitted, lowercase C identifier prefix derived package name. helper performs important substitutions, including @useDynLib, R_init_<Package>(), rsd_, RSD_, RC_ symbols. copied scaffold includes:

title: Using RsimdDispatch in Other Packages
text: demo operations collected backend operation table: real package, replace demo operation signatures keep structure. backend may set operation slot NULL operation deliberately unsupported backend; dispatched wrapper reports clear error explicit backend selection, "auto" skips backend unsupported operation. put -mavx2, -mavx512*, -msimd128, -march=native global package flags. configure helper keeps ISA flags local optional staged kernel objects, dispatcher remains safe baseline CPUs.
code: typedef struct RsdOps {     rsd_count_nonzero_fn count_nonzero;     rsd_convolve1d_fn convolve1d; } RsdOps; R API wrapper        ordinary src/Makevars compilation CPU feature checks   ordinary src/Makevars compilation dispatch table       ordinary src/Makevars compilation scalar kernel        staged by configure under src/rsd-kernels/ SSE/AVX/NEON/wasm files   staged by configure as optional objects under src/rsd-kernels/

title: Using RsimdDispatch in Other Packages
text: staged build model already scales across operations: add another function existing tools/kernels/kernel_*.c file, staged object backend contains new implementation. small number operations, keep dispatch code explicit add: function-pointer typedef RsdOps field src/simd_dispatch.h; one implementation per backend file tools/kernels/, following naming convention rsd_<operation>_<backend>(); backend declarations, RsdOps initializers using function pointers explicit NULL slots, dispatched wrapper src/simd_dispatch.c; .Call wrapper src/r_api.c; native registration entry src/registration.c; R wrapper, tests, documentation. Backend metadata table-driven, diagnostics auto-selection need second hand-maintained backend-name list. explicit layout easier read template still keeps backend-selection logic centralized. packages many public operations, keep single operation catalog can generate repetitive dispatch metadata, .Call registration, R wrappers. Optimized backend kernels can remain hand-written, operation names, argument lists, exported wrapper metadata duplicated hand.

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Pure-C runtime SIMD dispatch templates R packages. Stage scalar, SSE, AVX, AVX-512, NEON, WebAssembly SIMD128 kernel objects configuration; link one shared library switch among compiled CPU-supported implementations runtime.

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Copy C dispatch scaffold package root: helper updates DESCRIPTION LinkingTo: RsimdDispatch, writes scaffold files, substitutes package-specific registration C symbol prefixes. add runtime dependency RsimdDispatch. Replace demo count_nonzero() convolve1d() kernels tools/kernels/ scalar/SSE/AVX/NEON/WebAssembly kernels. generated configure script probes compiler support, stages selected kernel objects src/rsd-kernels/, leaves src/Makevars link objects baseline R API, CPU detection, dispatcher. See package vignettes downstream-package workflow dispatch semantics.

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: simd_set_backend() rejects uncompiled CPU-unsupported backends. Operation-level backend availability reported simd_info()$operation_backends.
code: library(RsimdDispatch)  x <- as.raw(c(0, 1, 2, 0, 255)) a <- c(1, 2, 3) b <- c(10, 100)  count_nonzero(x) #> [1] 3 convolve1d(a, b) #> [1]  10 120 230 300 simd_backend() #> [1] "avx2" simd_set_backend("scalar") simd_backend() #> [1] "scalar" simd_set_backend("avx2") simd_backend() #> [1] "avx2" simd_set_backend("auto") simd_info()[c("compiled_backends", "available_backends", "simde_native_backends")] #> $compiled_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   "avx512" #> #> $available_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2" #> #> $simde_native_backends #> [1] "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   "avx512" simde_info()[c("version", "commit")] #> $version #> [1] "0.8.4" #> #> $commit #> [1] "f3e8262173b7089db9a9d57a9ecef8dd07ad9c97" simd_dispatch_template_path() #> [1] "/usr/local/lib/R/site-library/RsimdDispatch/templates/dispatch-c"

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Switching backends -process variants linked one R shared library selected guarded operation table.
code: count_nonzero(x) #> [1] 3 convolve1d(a, b) #> [1]  10 120 230 300 simd_info()[c("selected_backend", "available_backends")] #> $selected_backend #> [1] "avx2" #> #> $available_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2" simd_set_backend("scalar") scalar_count <- count_nonzero(x) scalar_conv <- convolve1d(a, b)  simd_set_backend("avx2") avx2_count <- count_nonzero(x) avx2_conv <- convolve1d(a, b)  simd_set_backend("auto") data.frame(   scalar = scalar_count,   avx2 = avx2_count,   convolution_equal = identical(scalar_conv, avx2_conv),   selected_after_auto = simd_backend() ) #>   scalar avx2 convolution_equal selected_after_auto #> 1      3    3              TRUE                avx2

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: runtime switch can benchmark full one-dimensional convolution: classic nested-loop [+ j - 1] += [] * b[j] kernel.
code: set.seed(1) x <- as.raw(sample.int(256L, 50 * 1024 * 1024, replace = TRUE) - 1L)  mark <- bench::mark(   scalar = {     simd_set_backend("scalar")     count_nonzero(x)   },   avx2 = {     simd_set_backend("avx2")     count_nonzero(x)   },   iterations = 20,   check = TRUE,   memory = FALSE )  simd_set_backend("auto")  bench <- data.frame(   backend = as.character(mark$expression),   median_ms = as.numeric(mark$median) * 1000,   mb_per_second = length(x) * as.numeric(mark$`itr/sec`) / 1e6,   iterations = mark$n_itr,   row.names = NULL ) bench$speedup_vs_scalar <- bench$mb_per_second / bench$mb_per_second[bench$backend == "scalar"]  knitr::kable(bench, digits = 3) set.seed(2) a <- runif(100000) b <- runif(100)  conv_mark <- bench::mark(   scalar = {     simd_set_backend("scalar")     convolve1d(a, b)   },   avx2 = {     simd_set_backend("avx2")     convolve1d(a, b)   },   iterations = 20,   check = TRUE,   memory = FALSE )  simd_set_backend("auto")  conv_bench <- data.frame(   backend = as.character(conv_mark$expression),   median_ms = as.numeric(conv_mark$median) * 1000,   million_multiply_adds_per_second = (length(a) * length(b)) * as.numeric(conv_mark$`itr/sec`) / 1e6,   iterations = conv_mark$n_itr,   row.names = NULL ) conv_bench$speedup_vs_scalar <- conv_bench$million_multiply_adds_per_second /   conv_bench$million_multiply_adds_per_second[conv_bench$backend == "scalar"]  knitr::kable(conv_bench, digits = 3)

what: Full one-dimensional convolution with the selected SIMD backend — convolve1d
title: Full one-dimensional convolution with the selected SIMD backend — convolve1d
text: Demonstration numeric kernel runtime dispatch template. convolve1d() computes full convolution simple nested-loop R definition. pair positions adds [] * b[j] [+ j - 1]. SIMD backends vectorize inner multiply-add b shifted output window.

title: Report vendored SIMDe provenance — simde_info
RsimdDispatch 0.1.2.9000
text: Open post-0.1.2 development cycle. Clarify many-operation maintenance guidance: repetitive dispatch metadata, C registration, R wrapper metadata come one source truth; hand-written code focus backend kernels. Move downstream-use vignette runtime dispatch semantics pkgdown article order shorten many-operation guidance. Trim redundant backend-selection wording README dispatch semantics documentation. Move staged-kernel-kernel_common.h helper src/ tools/kernels/ copied template kernel directory, update downstream-template docs layout. Replace short-lived C preprocessor expansion blocks src/ explicit operation-table declarations, dispatch wrappers, native registration entries. Support explicit NULL operation slots backend/operation combinations deliberately unsupported, report operation-level backend availability simd_info(), make "auto" skip unsupported slots operation called.

title: RsimdDispatch 0.1.2
text: Add WebAssembly SIMD128 (wasm_simd128) staged-kernel support Emscripten/webR builds using SIMDe’s <simde/wasm/simd128.h> backend SIMDE_WASM_SIMD128_NATIVE configure probing. Extend simd_info() diagnostics cpu_wasm_simd128 include wasm_simd128 compiled, available, SIMDe-native backend sets built SIMD128-capable WebAssembly toolchain. Add webR check tooling based local rwasm::build() + Node webr workflow used related packages. Generate explicit SIMDe include flags src/Makevars quote configure include paths copied templates robust downstream libraries. Eagerly initialize dispatch package load, exercise copied-template runtime behavior CI, remove legacy comma-separated backend-list C helpers. Switch dispatch one function pointer per operation backend RsdOps operation table. Backend diagnostics now reuse C backend metadata table, R setter longer hardcodes backend names. Add dispatched convolve1d() full one-dimensional convolution demo, using SIMDe inner-loop multiply-add kernels numeric vectors, include tests, documentation, webR checks, evaluated benchmarks. Document current extension path adding operations retaining RsimdDispatch’s staged-object R package build model.

title: RsimdDispatch 0.1.1 (2026-05-28)
text: Stage scalar optional SIMD kernel objects configure, link generated src/Makevars baseline R API, CPU feature detection, dispatcher code. Move demo kernels src/ tools/kernels/ copied templates keep build-time kernel sources separate ordinary R package src/ files. Reword DESCRIPTION avoid incoming spell-check notes backend acronym names keeping detailed backend names user-facing documentation.

title: RsimdDispatch 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Provides working runtime SIMD dispatch example R packages, using one shared library scalar, SSE2, SSE4.1, AVX2, AVX-512, NEON kernel objects supported compiler CPU. Exports count_nonzero(), simd_set_backend(), simd_backend(), simd_info() demonstrate safe -process backend selection diagnostics. Adds use_simd_dispatch() simd_dispatch_template_path() package authors want copy adapt C dispatch scaffold. Vendors header-SIMDe library downstream packages LinkingTo: RsimdDispatch. Adds simde_info() SIMDe fields simd_info() report vendored SIMDe version pinned upstream commit.
